subject: Biology
title: Superbugs and Antibiotic Resistance
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Superbugs and Antibiotic Resistance.

hook.text: Bacteria are evolving faster than we can make antibiotics. We may be heading to a post-antibiotic era.

buildup.text: Antibiotic resistance occurs when bacteria mutate to survive drugs that once killed them.

discovery.text: MRSA, resistant to multiple drugs, kills more Americans each year than HIV.

twist.text: Overuse of antibiotics in farming—not medicine—drives most global resistance development.

climax.text: Without new strategies, routine surgeries and minor infections could become life-threatening again.

punchline.text: We gave bacteria our best weapons. They adapted.

quiz.question: What drives the most antibiotic resistance globally?
